title: Mouthwatering Gooey Indian Seafood Korma
description: A fresh and adventurous Indian dish featuring seafood stock, dehydrated ingredients, and a raw-friendly korma form, all wrapped in a corn husk vessel.
instructions: In a large pan, heat the ghee over medium heat and add the cumin, coriander, and garam masala.
Add the shrimp to the pan and cook until pink, about 2-3 minutes per side.
In a large bowl, whisk together the seafood stock and coconut milk.
Add the cooked shrimp to the bowl and stir to combine.
Spoon the mixture onto the center of a corn husk, leaving a 1 inch border around the edges.
Fold the sides of the corn husk over the filling and roll up the wrapper to form a tight cylinder.
Place the wrapped korma on a baking sheet and bake for 15-20 minutes, or until the filling is hot and the corn husk is crispy.
Serve hot, garnished with lemon juice.
